Arcanum
· The Extracurricular Marketplace ·
What is Arcanum?

Arcanum is an extracurricular marketplace and planning tool for high school students. Browse a curated catalog of ECs by category, difficulty, and real-world impact. Save the ones that fit your goals. Get a personalized roadmap from an AI advisor who asks the right questions. Track your progress from start to finish.

· The EC Marketplace ·

Your Future Starts
With The Right Spells

The first EC marketplace built for students, rated by students. Browse hundreds of real extracurriculars, build a personalized roadmap with your AI advisor, and track every step — all in one place.

HOW IT WORKS
From browsing to a real plan
01
Browse the catalog

Filter 3,000+ real extracurriculars by category, difficulty, time, and impact — every one rated by students who actually did it, not consultants.

02
Save what fits

Drop anything you're considering into your Saved list. No commitment — just build a shortlist as you explore.

03
Get a personalized plan

Tell the AI advisor your grade, goals, and what you're working with. It builds a roadmap around your exact situation — not generic "do more volunteering" advice.

04
Track every step

Move the ones you commit to into your Tracker and log status, milestones, and deadlines all the way through application season.

THE FOUR RATINGS
Every EC, scored on what matters
🔮
Essence

Overall value — how much this actually moves the needle with admissions.

🌿
Branches

Difficulty — how much skill, knowledge, or prep it genuinely takes.

🦴
Bones

Time commitment — the real hours per week, not the brochure version.

🍄
Mushrooms

Competitiveness — how hard it is to get in, place, or be selected.

WHO IT'S FOR
Built for students figuring it out

Whether you're a freshman with no clue where to start or a junior trying to tie a scattered profile together, Arcanum gives you the same thing: real information on what activities are actually worth your time, and a plan that fits your life — not someone else's. No counselor required, no cost, no catch.

Why I built this

I got tired of watching people around me pick extracurriculars by guessing and just hoping it'd work out. There's real info on what actually matters — it's just usually stuck behind expensive counselors. This is my attempt to put it in one free place that anyone can use.

✦ THE RATING SYSTEMS ✦
Three Ways to Judge a Spell
🔮
Essence — Overall Value

The umbrella rating. How much is this EC actually worth, all things considered? Balances difficulty, time, competition, and what it gets you. Shown on every card.

🌿
Branches — Difficulty

How hard is this EC? Covers skill required, mental demand, and barriers to success. Rated 0–5 in quarter steps by students who've done it.

🦴
Bones — Time Commitment

How many hours will this actually take? Weekly time, total duration, and whether you can realistically balance it with other commitments.

🍄
Mushrooms — Competitive Entry

How hard is it to get in? Acceptance rates, prerequisites, selection difficulty. A 5 means very few applicants make it.

✦ HOW TO NAVIGATE ✦
Every page in the Academy, explained.
⚔ THE STACKS

The Marketplace. Browse 3,000+ real extracurricular activities — filtered by category, difficulty, time, competition level, and rarity. Click any spell card to see the full detail page: four ratings, what you will actually do, time and cost estimates, and student reviews. Add anything you want to your Spellbook.

🔮 JEBADIAS

Jebadias Hawthorne — your AI advisor. Tell him your grade, goals, interests, and constraints. He asks real questions until he understands your specific situation, then builds a personalized EC roadmap. As you talk more, his personas deepen. There are nine total. There are also things that are not Jebadias. Explore the The Map to learn more.

📜 SPELLBOOK

Your wishlist. When you find an EC in the Sanctum that interests you, add it to your Spellbook. It saves automatically — no account needed. Your Spellbook is a staging area, not a commitment. Browse freely. Add generously. When you are ready to commit to something, Cast your Spellbook into your Grimoire.

📖 GRIMOIRE

Your active EC tracker. After you Cast your Spellbook, every EC moves here with a status (Not Started / In Progress / Done) and a milestone log. Record specific, verifiable accomplishments as they happen — dates, numbers, outcomes. By the time you are filling out your application, your Grimoire will have everything you need already written down.

✒ THE QUILL

Where you submit new spells to the Academy. If you have personally done an EC that is not in the Sanctum — or if you have done one that is listed and want to add your experience — you can submit it here. Rate it honestly. Describe what actually happened. Jebadias reviews all submissions. False listings are subject to investigation.

🗺 ARCANA

A map of the World of Arcana — the fictional world the Academy exists within. Click any location to read its lore. Certain locations unlock Chronicle Fragments, which appear in your Chronicle Vault. There are also things on the map that are not labeled, not obvious, and not entirely explained. The Academy encourages thorough exploration.

⚖ CODEX

The Academy's laws. Five sections covering listings, reviews, community conduct, consulting the Oracle, and consequences. Read the Codex before submitting a listing or writing a review. The rules exist because honest information is the entire point of Arcanum. Violations are taken seriously. Expulsion is permanent.

✦ THE LORE

The origin story of Arcanum — the problem it was built to solve, the solution it offers, how the four rating systems work, who Jebadias Hawthorne is, the lore layer that wraps the platform, and what is coming in Part Two. If you want to understand the Academy before you start exploring, begin here.

✦ EXPLORE BY CATEGORY ✦
40 categories. 3,000+ spells.
✦ THE HONEST GUIDE ✦
What actually makes an EC matter?
01 — DEPTH OVER BREADTH

Three ECs done seriously, documented honestly, and understood deeply will always outperform twelve ECs done at surface level. Admissions committees read activities lists carefully. They notice when everything is shallow.

02 — IMPACT IS VERIFIABLE

Raised $3,000 for a local shelter. Published two research papers. Reached 8,000 users. These are facts. "Contributed to the team's overall success" is not. Arcanum's Grimoire tracker is built to help you document specific, verifiable milestones as they happen.

03 — RARITY IS RELATIVE

A Common EC done exceptionally — leading a food bank with 200 volunteers, founding a tutoring program that reaches 50 students — is worth more than a Rare EC you participated in once. Use the Essence rating to find the ceiling, then build toward it.

04 — NARRATIVE WINS

The strongest applications have a through-line — a coherent story about who you are and what you build. Your ECs should reinforce a single narrative, not scatter across every possible category. Jebadias can help you find your thread and build around it.

05 — START EARLIER THAN YOU THINK

Junior year is when most students start thinking about ECs. The students who stand out started sophomore year or earlier — not because they were gaming the system, but because they had more time to build something real. Whatever grade you're in: now.

06 — HONEST RATINGS HELP EVERYONE

When you finish an EC, come back and rate it honestly. A 2/5 Bones rating that's accurate is more valuable than a 5/5 that isn't. The students who find this platform after you will make better decisions because of what you wrote. This is how the Arcanum works.

✦ YOUR ADVISOR ✦

Jebadias Hawthorne, Oracle of the Arcanum

He has been advising students for 237 years. He has seen every background, every goal, every mistake. He does not give generic advice. He asks you questions until he understands your specific situation, and then he builds a roadmap that fits your actual life.

Nine personas. Unlocked through genuine engagement. The deeper you go, the more he reveals — about your path, about the Arcanum, and about things that were buried a very long time ago.

"Tell me what you are trying to build. Not what you think I want to hear. What you are actually trying to build. We will go from there."
— Jebadias Hawthorne, Age 237

LIVE PREVIEW — how your EC card will look
List an extracurricular you've created, led, or participated in. Your listing helps other students find and evaluate real ECs — rated honestly by the students who've done them.

✦ WHO SHOULD LIST

Students who personally participated, led, or founded the EC. Not ones you researched but didn't do.

✦ WHAT MAKES IT GOOD

Specific descriptions. Honest ratings. Real time estimates. Details that you'd want before committing.

✦ AFTER SUBMISSION

All listings go through AI moderation. Approved ones appear in the Marketplace within 48 hours.

All listings go through AI moderation before appearing in the Marketplace. Inaccurate or misleading submissions will be rejected. See the Codex for guidelines.

Codex

Arcanum's community rules and guidelines. The Marketplace is only as valuable as its listings are accurate. The Codex is how we protect that.

"I will list only what I have done. I will rate only what I have experienced. I will help other students the way I would want to be helped — with honesty, without performance."

— The Arcanum Mage's Pledge

📋 LISTING RULES
01
List only ECs you have personally participated in, led, or founded. Do not list programs you've researched but not done — there is no value in secondhand information presented as first-hand experience.
02
Rate difficulty and time commitment based on your actual experience. Overrating to appear more impressive, or underrating to attract more participants, undermines the system for everyone.
03
Descriptions must reflect what participants actually do — not what the program's website or marketing materials claim. If the reality differed from the pitch, describe the reality.
04
Include accurate, current information about costs, prerequisites, and application deadlines where known. Outdated information is better omitted than guessed at.
05
Do not list fictional, fabricated, or significantly embellished ECs. Do not exaggerate your role, the program's selectivity, or the outcomes you achieved. All listings are subject to community review.
06
Do not use the Marketplace to advertise services, products, tutoring, or anything unrelated to genuine student extracurricular activities.
🤝 COMMUNITY RULES
07
Jebadias's advice is AI-generated and reflects general patterns, not guaranteed outcomes. Verify program details, deadlines, and eligibility requirements independently before committing. Arcanum is not responsible for outcomes based on AI recommendations.
08
Reviews must reflect genuine personal experience. Do not write reviews for ECs you haven't done, do not write fake positive reviews for ECs you want to promote, and do not write negative reviews for competitive programs you want to deter others from applying to.
09
Do not use Jebadias or any Arcanum feature to generate content intended to deceive colleges, fabricate experiences, or assist with academic dishonesty. Arcanum is a planning tool, not a ghostwriting service.
10
Treat the platform as you would want others to treat it. Every listing you contribute is read by real students making real decisions. The accuracy of what you write directly affects the quality of their choices.
MODERATION & APPEALS
11
All new listings are reviewed by AI moderation before appearing in the Marketplace. The system checks for accuracy, completeness, relevance, and compliance with these rules. Listings that fail moderation are not published.
12
Any listing in the Marketplace can be flagged by any user for community review. Flagged listings are reviewed by a human within 72 hours. Listings confirmed to violate the Codex are removed or corrected.
13
If your listing was rejected or removed and you believe the decision was incorrect, submit an appeal through the Contact page with the listing details and your reasoning. Appeals are reviewed by a human and receive a response within 72 hours. The decision after appeal is final.
14
Repeated violations of the Codex may result in permanent removal of all listings submitted by that user. Arcanum reserves the right to make this determination without notice.
🔮 A NOTE ON JEBADIAS
15
Jebadias Hawthorne is a fictional character. His lore, his history, and his personality are creative work, not factual claims about a real person. The advice he gives, however, is generated by a real AI model and intended to be genuinely useful. Treat the advice seriously. Treat the lore with the care it deserves.
16
The number sixteen recurs in the history of Arcana. If you have reached this rule, you have read the whole Codex. That is noted.
The Origin

What Arcanum is, why it was built, and what it's trying to fix.

THE PROBLEM

Here's the thing nobody really tells you: most people fill out the activities section of their college app by basically guessing. You list what you did, cram it into 150 characters, and hope it was enough. Nobody explains what admissions officers are actually looking for, or which activities are worth your time versus which ones kind of don't matter, or how to make your profile hang together as one story. That info exists — it's just stuck behind expensive counselors and inside the heads of kids who went to schools that teach this stuff. That always bugged me.

THE SOLUTION

So Arcanum is a catalog of extracurriculars where the ratings come from students who actually did them, not some algorithm guessing. Each one gets scored on four things that actually matter — how meaningful it is, how hard it is, how much time it eats, and how competitive it is to get into — so you can filter for what fits your life. And the advisor, Jebadias, doesn't just say "do more community service." You tell him your grade, your goals, and what you're working with, and he builds an actual plan around you.

WHO BUILT THIS

I'm a high schooler in Charlotte, NC, and I built this because I was tired of watching people around me — me included — pick extracurriculars at random and just hope something stuck. I wanted something that gave real signal instead of noise. That's pretty much the whole reason it exists.

THE WORLD OF ARCANA

Okay, the whole fantasy thing — the world, the map, Jebadias, the Chronicle — it's not some deep metaphor or anything. Honestly, planning your future can feel intimidating and kind of overwhelming, especially at 15 when everyone acts like every choice is life-or-death. The fantasy layer is just there to make it feel less like a scary spreadsheet and more like something you actually want to poke around in. It's completely optional — flip on Planar mode and it all disappears. The advice underneath is identical either way.

HOW IT'S BUILT · DATA · AI

Arcanum is a single-page web app I built and deployed myself. The catalog, the tracker, and all the logic live in one file, with a Cloudflare Worker proxying the AI requests and Supabase handling optional accounts and sync.

On AI, straight up: Jebadias is powered by Anthropic's Claude. The sample reviews on activities are AI-generated to show the kind of feedback something tends to get — they're labeled as samples, not real student quotes. The four ratings are illustrative starting points, not survey data. Anything the AI drafts — the Common App export, the master plan — is a draft for you to edit, never a finished answer. I used AI to help build parts of this too, and I can explain how every piece works.

On your data: everything saves to your own browser by default and never leaves it unless you make an account — in which case it syncs to a private database so it follows you across devices. No ads. No trackers. Nothing sold. Ever.

3,000+
ECs rated by students
40
Categories covered
Free
Always and forever
WHAT ARCANUM IS NOT

Arcanum is not a guarantee. It is not a college counselor. It is not a substitute for actually doing the work, following through on what you commit to, or being honest about who you are. Admissions is not a formula. No app, advisor, or database can tell you what will work for your specific situation at your specific schools in your specific year. What Arcanum can do is help you stop guessing — and start building something real.

The Guide

Answers to every question about Arcanum, the ratings, Jebadias, and the world.

What is Arcanum?▼

Arcanum is a peer-reviewed extracurricular marketplace for high school students. Every EC in The Stacks was added and rated by students who actually did it. The four rating dimensions let you filter by what matters — overall value, difficulty, time commitment, and how competitive the application process is — instead of guessing from a blank list.

What do the four ratings mean?▼

🔮 Essence — Overall value. Does this activity move the needle with admissions?

🌿 Branches — Difficulty. How much skill, knowledge, or preparation does it genuinely require?

🦴 Bones — Time commitment. How many hours per week does it realistically demand?

🍄 Mushrooms — Competitive entry. How hard is it to get in, place, or be selected? A 5 means under 5% acceptance.

Who is Jebadias?▼

Jebadias Hawthorne is the Academy's advisor. He is not a general-purpose AI. He asks targeted questions about your grade, goals, interests, time, and constraints — then builds a personalized EC roadmap for your situation. He has nine personas spanning his 237-year history, from his first months as an advisor at age 23 to the Oracle he becomes centuries later. They unlock as you explore.

What is the difference between Spellbook and Grimoire?▼

Spellbook is your wishlist. Save any EC you're considering without committing. Browse freely. Add generously. Your Spellbook persists across sessions automatically.

Grimoire is your active tracker. When you commit to pursuing an EC, cast it from your Spellbook into your Grimoire. Log milestones, track status, and record outcomes. By application time, your Grimoire will contain everything you need to write your activities section.

What are Chronicle fragments?▼

Chronicle fragments are pieces of hidden lore scattered across the World of Arcana. Click locations on the map to discover them. Some are found immediately. Some require clicking the same location multiple times. Some require finding things that are not labeled. The Chronicle page shows your collection and the story they form when read together.

Why is there a fantasy world layer?▼

Mostly because planning your future is stressful, and a plain spreadsheet of "things you should do" makes it feel even more intimidating. The world, the map, the advisor, the lore — it's all just there to make the whole thing feel lighter and more fun to actually engage with, especially if you're younger and the college stuff feels overwhelming. None of it is required. If it's not for you, switch to Planar mode in Settings and it all goes away — the planning tools and advice are exactly the same underneath.

How do the filters in The Stacks work?▼

Filters are multi-select. Click any category to filter by it. Click multiple categories to see ECs across all of them. The impact and rarity rows work the same way. Click a selected filter again to deselect it. "All" resets everything. The sort dropdown sorts the filtered results by whichever dimension matters most to you right now.

What's the difference between Saved and Tracker?▼

Saved is your shortlist — anything you're considering, no commitment. Tracker is for the ones you've actually committed to: you log status, milestones, and deadlines there. Basically, browse and dump stuff into Saved, then move the real ones into Tracker once you decide to do them.

PLANNING & STRATEGY
How many extracurriculars should I actually have?▼

Fewer than you think. The Common App gives you ten slots, but stuffing all ten with random clubs is weaker than four or five things you genuinely went deep on. Colleges care way more about commitment and what you actually did than a long list of stuff you showed up to twice.

What's a "spike" and do I need one?▼

A spike is just being really good at or known for one thing instead of being kind of average at a bunch. It's not mandatory, but a clear spike with one or two other interests around it tends to read stronger than a profile that's all over the place. Use Your Journey (under Explore) to see if your saved ECs are leaning one way or scattered.

I'm a freshman or sophomore — is it too early?▼

Honestly the opposite — earlier is better. Not because you need to grind at 14, but because starting early means you can try stuff, drop what you hate, and have time to actually get good at what you keep. No pressure to have it figured out. Just start poking around.

Do colleges actually care about extracurriculars?▼

Yeah, at most schools that read applications holistically. Grades and test scores usually come first, but once you're in the range, your activities are a big part of how they tell you apart from everyone else with the same GPA. It's where they see who you actually are.

What if I can't afford expensive programs?▼

Then skip them — seriously. A ton of the most impressive stuff costs nothing: starting something at your school, organizing in your community, self-teaching a skill and building a project. Admissions readers care about what you did with what you had, not how pricey the program was. You can filter The Stacks by cost to find free options.

How do I make a normal activity actually stand out?▼

Take initiative and leave it better than you found it. Start something, lead something, hit a number you can point to. "Member of the club" is forgettable; "ran the club's first fundraiser and raised $2k" is not. Same activity, totally different story.

RATINGS & CATALOG
Where do the ratings actually come from?▼

From students who've done the activity, not some formula. They're meant to give you a realistic starting feel for how hard, time-heavy, and competitive something is — directional, not gospel. Read the reviews on a card to get the fuller picture.

The ratings feel subjective — how much should I trust them?▼

Treat them like a friend's honest opinion, not a final verdict. They're great for narrowing down and comparing, but your situation is yours. If a "hard" EC genuinely excites you, that matters more than the number.

An EC I did isn't in here — can I add it?▼

Please do. Head to Submit (the Quill), fill it out honestly with real ratings, and it goes through a quick AI check before it's added so the catalog stays useful for everyone.

Can I trust the reviews on each EC?▼

They're written from a student's-eye view, so read a few and look for patterns rather than fixating on one. If three people all say "huge time commitment," believe them.

ACCOUNTS & DATA
Do I need to make an account?▼

Nope. Everything works without one and saves to your device automatically. The only reason to sign up is if you want your stuff to sync across your phone and laptop — then hit Sign in at the top right and make a free account.

Is my data private?▼

By default it never leaves your browser. If you make an account it syncs to a secure database (Supabase) so it follows you between devices. Either way, your stuff isn't sold or handed off to anyone — this isn't that kind of project.

What happens if I clear my browser?▼

If you're not signed in, clearing browser data wipes your saved stuff — so if you've put real work in, make an account. With an account, your data lives in the cloud and comes right back when you log in.

THE PROJECT
Who made this?▼

A high schooler in Charlotte, NC, who was tired of watching people pick extracurriculars by guessing. There's more on the About page if you're curious.

Is this affiliated with a college or the Common App?▼

No — it's an independent student project, not connected to any college, the Common App, or any company. Just a tool built to be genuinely helpful.

Is it really free, like actually?▼

Actually free. No ads, no premium tier, no "upgrade to unlock." Everything's open to everyone, and that's not changing.

How do I unlock the advisor's other personas?▼

Just keep talking to Jebadias and exploring the world — his different eras unlock as you go. They change his personality and the stories he tells, never the quality of the advice.

Do I have to use the fantasy stuff?▼

Not at all. Open Settings and turn off Game mode — you get a clean, plain version with normal names (Marketplace, Advisor, Tracker) and zero lore. Every feature stays; only the styling changes.

MORE STRATEGY
I have no idea what I'm into — where do I even start?▼

Start with the "Find my starting path" quiz on the home page — a few quick questions and it points you at categories that actually fit you. From there just browse and save whatever sounds even slightly interesting. You don't need a master plan on day one.

What's the single best EC for getting into a top school?▼

There isn't one, and anybody selling you "the" magic activity is lying. Top schools want depth and authenticity, not a specific checkbox. The "best" EC is the one you'll actually stick with and do something real in.

Can good extracurriculars make up for a lower GPA?▼

Somewhat, but be realistic — grades and rigor usually come first. Standout activities can tip you over the edge once you're in a school's academic range, but they rarely rescue an application on their own. Do both.

How do I balance all this with actually keeping my grades up?▼

Don't over-commit. Two or three things you care about, done well, beats ten you're drowning in. Use the time-commitment rating (🦴 Bones) on each EC to be honest with yourself before you sign up for something that eats your whole week.

Should my activities match my intended major?▼

It helps to have a clear thread, but you don't have to be a robot about it. A strong "spike" toward your interest plus one or two things you just genuinely enjoy reads as a real person, which is the point.

Is it bad if I switch interests partway through high school?▼

Not at all — that's normal and even healthy. What matters is that wherever you land, you go deep. A clear pivot you can explain ("tried X, found Y, ran with it") is way better than fake consistency.

What do the impact levels (Local → Global) actually mean?▼

They describe how far an activity's reach goes — from your school or town (Local) all the way up to national or international (Global). Higher isn't automatically better; a deep local project often beats a shallow "global" one. It's just there to help you aim.

Do summer programs actually matter?▼

The competitive, selective ones can be a strong signal. The pay-to-attend ones that admit anyone with a check? Much less so. Filter by competitiveness (🍄 Mushrooms) and don't assume an expensive program automatically impresses anyone.

My school barely has any clubs — what do I do?▼

Honestly? That's an opportunity. Starting something where there's a gap — a club, a project, a small initiative — is often more impressive than joining one that already exists. A lot of the catalog is stuff you can launch yourself.

How do I find opportunities actually near me?▼

A lot of the strongest ECs are local — volunteering, internships, community projects — and a quick search for your city plus the activity usually turns them up. Use the catalog to find the type of thing you want, then go hunt for the local version of it.

Contact

Reach out with bug reports, listing disputes, feature requests, or anything else. Every message is read by a human.

All messages receive a human response within 48–72 hours. Complex appeals may take longer.

RESPONSE TIMES

Bug reports: 24–48 hours

Listing disputes: 48–72 hours

Appeals: Up to 5 business days

Everything else: 48–72 hours

GOOD TO KNOW

For new EC submissions, use the The Quill page — not Contact.

For Jebadias issues, try clearing your chat history in Settings first.

For lore questions — you're on your own. That's kind of the point.

DIRECT EMAIL
prithivivijayakumar.work@gmail.com

Support Send a Raven
LOADING ARCANUM...
🏪
BROWSE THE MARKETPLACE

A catalog of 3,208 real extracurriculars, each rated 0–5 by students who actually did them across four dimensions: Essence (personal meaning), Branches (difficulty), Bones (time commitment), and Mushrooms (growth). Filter by category, rarity, impact (local → global), time, and cost. Tap any card for a full detail page — radar chart, step-by-step how-to, and what admissions readers actually look for.

📖
SAVE & TRACK

Tap + Save on any EC to add it to your Spellbook — your shortlist. When you commit to actually doing one, cast it into your Grimoire, your tracker, where you log status, milestones, and deadlines. Both save to your device automatically — no account, no sign-up. Everything stays private in your browser.

🔮
ASK THE ADVISOR

Jebadias is your AI advisor. Tell him your grade, goals, and interests and he builds a roadmap for your exact situation. In his chat, use Load Grimoire / Load Spellbook to hand him your whole portfolio for specific, honest feedback. He has nine "eras" you unlock by talking with him — they change his personality and the stories he tells, never the quality of his advice.

🗺️
EXPLORE THE WORLD

Arcanum is wrapped in a world — the island of Arcana. Open the Arcana map and click locations to uncover their stories and discover the eight chapters of The Chronicle, the hidden life story of your advisor. Some places aren't labeled. Some respond to persistence. It's all optional — pure exploration, with no effect on the planning tool itself.

⚙️
PREFER IT PLAIN?

Not into the fantasy? Open Settings (the Chamber) and switch off Game mode — right under the dark-mode toggle. Planar mode strips the lore, map, and arcade styling into a clean, minimal app with plain page names (Marketplace, Advisor, Tracker…). Every feature stays; only the look changes. Switch back anytime.

Tip: hover any item in the top menu to see what it does. Full walkthrough on the Home page or in The Guide.

✦
SAVE YOUR PROGRESS

Everything saves to your device on its own — but if you want it to follow you between your phone and laptop, make a free account. Hit Sign in up in the top-right corner, choose Create one, and that's it: your Spellbook, Grimoire, and progress all sync to the cloud. Totally optional — the whole thing works without it.

Skip
🔗 SHARE YOUR PLAN

Anyone with this link sees a read-only copy of your committed ECs — perfect for a parent, counselor, or mentor. The link carries the data itself, so nothing is stored on a server.

📋 COMMON APP EXPORT

Your committed ECs, drafted into ready-to-paste Common App Activities entries — trimmed to the real character limits (Position ≤50, Description ≤150).

These are AI-drafted starting points from your tracker, not finished submissions. Edit them into your own voice before you paste.

Explore

A few ways to wander the catalog.

📖 THE CHRONICLE — IN ORDER

Every chapter you've discovered, read end to end. Undiscovered chapters show as shadows.

📊 YOUR JOURNEY

A snapshot of everything you've built in Arcanum so far.

🧙
JEBADIAS
COMPARE 0/3
⚖ COMPARE ECs
✦ BY RARITY

Every EC, sorted by tier — Legendary down to Common.

📅 YOUR TIMELINE

Committed ECs across the months, by target date.

✦ THE ASCENSION ✦

The Ascension

Everything you've committed to, made personal. Open any card for a breakdown built around you — and when you're ready, let Jebadias read the whole picture at once.

Jebadias's Master Plan

When your Grimoire holds enough, Jebadias reads all of it at once and returns a full strategic debrief — narrative, gaps, your Common App section, a timeline, essay angles, and his own letter.

Jebadias is reading your Grimoire…
Tracing the pattern across everything you've committed to.
✦

Welcome to the Ascension

You've crossed from discovery into commitment. This is the Ascension — every EC you've committed to lives here as a card. Tap any card for a breakdown built around your goals. When you're ready, have Jebadias read the whole picture and write your master plan.

To add more, return and add ECs to your Spellbook, then come back. You'll stay here until you choose to leave — from the button up top or from Settings.

✦ PERSONALIZED FOR YOU

Make a free account so your Spellbook, Grimoire, and progress follow you everywhere.
